prompts: Centripetal force is the force required to keep an object moving in a circular path. It acts perpendicular to the object's velocity and points toward the center of the circle. In the context of circular motion, centripetal force can be seen in scenarios like a car turning around a curve or a roller coaster looping. This force is necessary to change the direction of the object without altering its speed. The magnitude of centripetal force can be calculated using the formula: F = mv²/r, where m is mass, v is velocity, and r is the radius of the circle.
